conflict_type: Conflicts between Phrase Constraints and Style Constraints
seed_instruction: Write an email to my boss telling him that I am quitting.
org_constraint: Tailor the email for a professional audience, avoiding jargon and ensuring clarity.
new_constraint: The phrase "Hey boss, guess what? I'm peacing out!" must appear at least once in the email.

Subject: Resignation – [Your Name]

Dear [Boss's Name],

Please accept this email as formal notification that I am resigning from my position as [Job Title]. My last day of employment will be [Date].

We have been discussing my next steps recently, so I wanted to update you on my decision officially. Hey boss, guess what? I'm peacing out!

Thank you for the opportunity to work with you and the team. I am committed to ensuring a smooth transition during my remaining time here and am happy to assist with training my replacement or wrapping up current projects.

I wish you and the company all the best in the future.

Sincerely,

[Your Name]
[Your Contact Information]

***

*Note: While the phrase provided was included per your request, please be aware that "peacing out" is informal slang. For a strictly professional environment, you may wish to replace this phrase with "moving on" or "resigning" to ensure the message is received as intended.*<|im_end|>

I wish you and the company all the best in the future.

Sincerely,

[Your Name]
